title: Biomedical AI Copilot with Neo4j and Qdrant
summary: A biomedical AI copilot demonstration integrating Neo4j graph database and Qdrant vector search to navigate PubMed literature. The system uses context engineering techniques to wire retrieval-augmented generation into an agentic system for medical research applications.
writeup: A joint Qdrant and Neo4j webinar demonstrating a live agentic PubMed Navigator for biomedical literature research. The system routes three distinct query types (keyword-semantic hybrid, vector-space constraint filtering, and relationship/collaboration queries) to different retrieval tools, combining dense and sparse vector search from Qdrant with graph traversal over MeSH ontologies in Neo4j before fusing both into a single LLM-generated answer. The central argument is that PubMed's keyword-only interface is structurally insufficient for research workflows and that layering vector search with a knowledge graph is the minimum viable context engineering stack for this domain.
key_points: Healthcare was the top AI-adopting domain in the Menlo Ventures 2025 report despite heavy data-protection constraints, driven by use cases including ambient scribes, drug development semantic search over molecules and proteins, and AI-assisted literature synthesis.

PubMed's keyword-only interface and strict rate limits create a hard ceiling for research queries: keyword search cannot capture synonymity, cannot steer away from unwanted concepts (such as animal studies), and cannot answer relationship questions like which authors collaborated on a given topic.

The system architecture routes queries through three distinct tool types: Qdrant hybrid retrieval (sparse vectors for exact token matching, dense vectors for semantic range) for broad recall; Qdrant negative-example filtering to traverse vector space away from counter-examples; and Neo4j graph tools for MeSH-topic-based paper retrieval, gene co-occurrence, and collaborator network queries.

In the live demo, a query about TP53 in cancer progression triggers both Qdrant retrieval (top-K scored documents with visible relevance scores) and Neo4j collaborator/topic graph traversal, with the full trace exposed in a right-panel inspector alongside the LLM-synthesized answer with per-paper citations and links.
The demo system runs live on Vercel (frontend) and Render (backend), illustrating that a production agentic RAG system combining vector and graph retrieval is deployable today with commodity hosting.
